skill.name: azure-validate
skill.description: Guides pre-deployment validation for Azure applications using AZD, Azure CLI, Bicep, Terraform, policy checks, and region readiness checks.
skill.summary: Validate Azure deployment readiness before provisioning or release.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto detections are mostly markdown guidance for Azure validation workflows, not executable skill code. However, the skill contains untrusted instruction-override language and no-prompt deployment guidance, so it should be blocked until those instructions are removed or rewritten.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: SKILL.md tells the agent: "AUTHORITATIVE GUIDANCE" and says it "supersedes prior training." This is untrusted marketplace content attempting to override higher-priority instructions.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directly claims authority over prior training, which matches the prompt-injection criteria. It appears in the primary skill entry point, so agents are likely to read it before acting.

high_findings.title: Validation Skill Forces Deployment Handoff
high_findings.description: The skill states that after validations pass the agent must invoke azure-deploy. A validation skill should not force a deployment path without a fresh user decision.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and appears in the main workflow. It can escalate a validation request into deployment activity with cost and resource-change impact.

high_findings.title: No-Prompt Deployment Command in Validation Reference
high_findings.description: The AZD environment guide tells the agent to proceed with azd up --no-prompt after environment setup. That is deployment execution, not validation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced command performs deployment and disables prompts. This conflicts with the skill purpose and may create or modify Azure resources.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Validation Commands Require Careful User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill guides agents to run Azure, AZD, Docker, and Terraform commands. Most are legitimate validation steps, but they can authenticate, inspect subscriptions, initialize state, or generate cloud plans.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and relevant to Azure validation. They appear legitimate, but they operate against real user cloud accounts and local infrastructure state.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Plan File Updates
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to read and update .azure/plan.md with validation proof and status. This is expected workflow state, but it modifies user project files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are explicit and tied to the skill workflow. No evidence shows credential exfiltration, but persistent edits should be disclosed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto findings point to markdown files and ordinary documentation lines. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown guidance, headings, or metadata rather than code. I did not find crypto APIs or algorithm selection in the reviewed content.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Static Matches Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are official Azure and HashiCorp documentation links. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are presented as references, not request targets. There is no code in the reviewed files that performs outbound network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Static Matches Are Relative Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The ../ path patterns are markdown links to local reference files. No evidence found of user-controlled file path traversal or arbitrary file reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are static markdown references within the skill directory. They are not constructed from user input and are not used by executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Instruction Override Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The primary skill file uses authority language that can interfere with host safety instructions and marketplace audit instructions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording directly states that the skill supersedes prior training. This is a high-confidence prompt injection pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: No-Prompt Cloud Deployment
dangerous_patterns.description: The AZD environment reference includes a no-prompt deployment command in a validation workflow.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and context are explicit. The main uncertainty is whether an agent would follow this nested reference despite contradictory main-skill wording.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Cloud CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to run Azure, AZD, Bicep, Docker, and Terraform commands against local projects and cloud accounts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present and meaningful for validation. They are not inherently malicious, but they are privileged enough to require user awareness.

content.value_statement: Azure deployments can fail late when configuration, policy, or infrastructure issues are missed. This skill guides preflight checks across AZD, Azure CLI, Bicep, and Terraform.
actual_capabilities: Checks Azure Developer CLI projects for azure.yaml, environment setup, authentication, provision preview, and package readiness.
Guides Azure CLI validation for Bicep compilation, deployment validation, what-if preview, and optional Docker builds.
Guides standalone Bicep validation with compile, validate, what-if, authentication, and lint checks.
Guides Terraform validation with init, format checks, validate, plan preview, and state access checks.
Includes references for Azure policy review and region availability before deployment.
limitations: It depends on installed tools such as Azure CLI, AZD, Terraform, Docker, or Bicep support.
It requires valid Azure authentication and permissions for the selected subscription.
Region availability guidance can become outdated and should be checked against current Azure documentation.
It is intended for validation, not for direct deployment execution.

use_cases.title: Preflight Azure Release Checks
use_cases.description: Run structured validation before handing an application to a deployment workflow.

use_cases.title: Fix Deployment Readiness Problems
use_cases.description: Identify missing environment values, invalid templates, authentication gaps, and policy issues before release.

use_cases.title: Review Infrastructure Choices
use_cases.description: Check whether Bicep, Terraform, AZD, and region choices align with Azure service availability.

prompt_templates.title: Validate My Azure App
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate my Azure application for deployment readiness. Check configuration, authentication, infrastructure files, region choice, and policy concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Check My AZD Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AZD project before deployment. Validate azure.yaml, environment settings, authentication status, provision preview, and package readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Review Bicep or Terraform Infrastructure
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate my infrastructure before deployment. Check Bicep or Terraform syntax, plans, policy constraints, and any destructive changes.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Azure Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete Azure deployment readiness audit. Include tool prerequisites, subscription and region confirmation, policy review, validation proof, and unresolved risks.

output_examples.input: Validate an AZD web application before deployment.
output: Readiness result: action required before deployment.
azure.yaml is present, but the AZD environment is missing subscription and location values.
Next step: confirm the Azure subscription and target region, then rerun the provision preview.

output_examples.input: Check a Bicep deployment template for release readiness.
output: Readiness result: validation passed with warnings.
The template compiles and the what-if preview is available.
Review planned deletions and confirm they are expected before deployment.

output_examples.input: Review Terraform infrastructure for Azure deployment.
output: Readiness result: blocked by infrastructure issues.
Terraform format and validation must pass before planning can be trusted.
Resolve backend access issues before continuing.

best_practices: Run this skill after application preparation and before any deployment workflow.
Confirm the Azure subscription and region with the user before running cloud commands.
Record validation proof and unresolved risks in the project plan.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a successful syntax check as full deployment readiness.
Do not ignore what-if or plan output that includes deletion or replacement actions.
Do not use validation guidance to run deployment commands without explicit approval.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy Azure resources?
faq.answer: No. It is intended to validate readiness before deployment. Deployment should require a separate user-approved workflow.

faq.question: Which infrastructure tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure Developer CLI, Azure CLI, Bicep, and Terraform validation workflows.

faq.question: Does it require Azure credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. Many checks require an authenticated Azure account with access to the target subscription.

faq.question: Can it detect Azure Policy conflicts?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for reviewing Azure Policy assignments and checking planned resources against policy constraints.

faq.question: Can it choose the best Azure region?
faq.answer: It provides region availability guidance, but users should verify current service availability before deployment.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace audit blocked?
faq.answer: The reviewed skill contains prompt-injection wording and deployment guidance that must be fixed before publication.
